person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography
Occupation:«/b» Yeoman, Sheep Farmer

"In 1558 John Buswell, yeoman and sheep farmer, was mentioned as a landholder in the parish of Clipston, co. Northampton, England.

In 1564 he was one of four men taxed in the parish of Dishley, co. Leicester. The Clipston parish registers before 1667 and the Dishley parish registers before 1681 have been lost, although there are scattered Bishop's Transcripts for Dishley after 1578. He is not named in visitation pedigrees of any county.

Thompson says the children were "all likely born at Dishley, co. Leicester."

The will of John Buswell of Dishley Grange, yeoman, is dated 5 March 16 Elizabeth (1573/1574) and proved in the Prerogative Court of Canterbury 19 May 1574. Wife Mary and son James Buswell were named executors, with Thomas Buswell (his eldest son) and Edward Turner as overseers. The will provided for his wife and five adult children (Thomas, William, Roger, James and Elizabeth), and four children under age (George, John, Clement, and Patience), and mentions his sister Agnes Wayd. Eldest son Thomas contested the will, which benefitted him little, but it was confirmed by a "Sentence" dated 19 February 1590/1591. The Sentence confirmed that widow Mary, "Buswell alias Goodall" and her husband Richard Goodall were still living."

Family
John Buswell married Elizabeth Hall about 1540, probably in Dishley Gr, Leicestershire, England.

John Buswell married Mary Marie Botham Of Clipston, Northamptonshire, England ABT 1554.

1.) Thomas Buswell

2.) William Buswell

3.) Roger Buswell

4.) James Buswell

5.) Elizabeth Buswell

Note that various land deeds involving George Buswell "of Derby" about 20 miles from Dishley/Dixeley appear at the UK National Archives in the early and mid 1600s along with one for Elias Buswell, very possibly the son and grand-son of this John Buswell respectively.

«b»The 1573 will of John Buswell of Dixeley«/b»
A will of John Buswell of Dixeley (Dishley, Leicestershire) was written on 5 March 1573 and probated 19 May 1574, some extracted text below. A transcription of the entire will is available online.

IN THE NAME OF GOD AMEN the fifte day of Marche In the sixtenthe yere of the raign of our soveraign Lady Elizabeth by the grace of god of England Fraunce and Ireland quene defender of the faithe & c And in the yere of lorde god a thousand fyve hundred threscore and thirtene I John Buswell of Dixeley Grange in the county of Leic' yoman being sick in body and of good and perfecte memorye...unto Mary Buswell my welbeloved wife All those my messuages milne lands tenementes ...unto Thomas Buswell my eldest sonne...to my fower sonnes Thomas, William, Roger, and James and the third part thereof I give unto my other three sonnes George John and Clement...to Elizabeth my dawghter twentie pounds...unto my daughter Patiens, tenne pounds to be paid in money or money worthe at the age of seventene yeres...unto my thre yonger sonnes George John and Clement to be paid at the age of one and twentie yeres...I give to Agnes Wayd my sister

The separation of sons seems to imply that they had different mothers? Thomas and James were executor and supervisor so were over 21. Some conflict arose in the execution of the will with Thomas on one side and James and his mother Mary, now married to notary public Richard Goodall on the other. Mary contended that John was of sound mind & memory when he wrote the will and the judge (William Lewin) said that Thomas had put forth nothing contradicting this view, so found in the favor of Mary and James on 19 Feb 1590/91.

nameMeanings.name: Buswell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Midlands): variant of   Boswell  , of Norman origin.

variants.name: Boswell
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Beuzeville-la-Giffard in Seine-Maritime, France, named with the ancient Germanic personal name Boso + ville ‘settlement’. The final element has been altered as a result of association with the common placename ending -well ‘spring, stream’. Some instances of Bovill may have the same origin.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: John Buswell was born about 1515, in England. He married Mrs. John Buswell in 1540, in Dishley, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died from 6 March 1574 to 19 May 1574, in Dishley, Leicestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: John Buswell est né environ 1515, à Angleterre. Il a épousé Mrs. John Buswell en 1540, à Dishley, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé du 6 mars 1574 au 19 mai 1574, à Dishley, Leicestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: John Buswell wurde um 1515 in England geboren. Er hat 1540 in Dishley, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom Mrs. John Buswell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb von 6. März 1574 bis 19. Mai 1574 in Dishley, Leicestershire, England.

it.lifeSummary: John Buswell è nato il circa 1515 nel England. Lui sposato Mrs. John Buswell nel 1540, nel Dishley, Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì da 6 marzo 1574 a 19 maggio 1574, nel Dishley, Leicestershire, England.
